title: Solemnity of Mary, Mother of God
description: Holy day honoring the Blessed Virgin Mary as Mother of God.

title: Epiphany of the Lord
description: Manifestation of Christ to the nations.

title: Baptism of the Lord
description: Commemorates the baptism of Jesus in the Jordan River.

title: Conversion of Saint Paul the Apostle
description: Feast of the Apostle to the Gentiles.

title: Presentation of the Lord
description: Also known as Candlemas; the presentation of Jesus in the Temple.

title: Saints Cyril and Methodius
description: Patrons of Europe and evangelizers of the Slavic peoples.

title: Chair of Saint Peter the Apostle
description: Celebrates the pastoral office of the Pope and unity of the Church.

title: Saint Matthias, Apostle
description: Apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot.

title: Saint Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Patron of the Universal Church and foster father of Jesus.

title: Annunciation of the Lord
description: The angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will conceive the Son of God.

title: Saint Mark, Evangelist
description: Author of the Gospel of Mark.

title: Saint Catherine of Siena, Virgin and Doctor of the Church
description: Doctor of the Church and patron of Italy and Europe.

title: Saint Joseph the Worker
description: Honors Saint Joseph as a model of dignified labor.

title: Saints Philip and James, Apostles
description: Feast of two of the Twelve Apostles.

title: Our Lady of Fatima
description: Commemorates the apparitions of the Blessed Virgin Mary at Fatima, Portugal.

title: Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Mary visits her cousin Elizabeth.

title: Nativity of Saint John the Baptist
description: Birth of the forerunner of the Lord.

title: Saint Anthony of Padua, Priest and Doctor of the Church
description: Franciscan preacher and Doctor of the Church.

title: Saints Peter and Paul, Apostles
description: Principal patrons of the Church of Rome.

title: First Martyrs of the Holy Roman Church
description: Commemorates the Christians martyred under Nero after Saint Peter and Paul.

title: Independence Day (Philippines)
description: National day often celebrated with Mass and prayers for the nation.

title: Saint Bonaventure, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
description: Franciscan theologian and Doctor of the Church.

title: Our Lady of Mount Carmel
description: Patroness of the Carmelite Order.

title: Saint James, Apostle
description: Apostle and martyr, patron of Spain.

title: Saints Joachim and Anne, Parents of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Grandparents of Jesus and parents of Mary.

title: Saint Martha
description: Friend of Jesus who served him in Bethany.

title: Saint Ignatius of Loyola, Priest
description: Founder of the Society of Jesus (Jesuits).

title: Saint Alphonsus Liguori, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
description: Founder of the Redemptorists and moral theologian.

title: Transfiguration of the Lord
description: Christ is transfigured before Peter, James, and John.

title: Saint Dominic, Priest
description: Founder of the Order of Preachers (Dominicans).

title: Saint Lawrence, Deacon and Martyr
description: Archdeacon of Rome and martyr.

title: Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Mary is taken body and soul into heavenly glory.

title: Saint Maximilian Kolbe, Priest and Martyr
description: Franciscan friar who gave his life at Auschwitz.

title: Saint Bartholomew, Apostle
description: Apostle and martyr.

title: Passion of Saint John the Baptist
description: Martyrdom of the forerunner of the Lord.

title: Birth of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Celebrates the nativity of the Mother of God.

title: Most Holy Name of Mary
description: Honors the name of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

title: Exaltation of the Holy Cross
description: Commemorates the finding and exaltation of the Cross of Christ.

title: Our Lady of Sorrows
description: Commemorates the sorrows of Mary at the foot of the Cross.

title: Saints Cornelius, Pope, and Cyprian, Bishop, Martyrs
description: Third-century martyrs and pastors of the Church.

title: Saint Matthew, Apostle and Evangelist
description: Apostle and author of the Gospel of Matthew.

title: Saints Cosmas and Damian, Martyrs
description: Physician martyrs and patrons of healthcare workers.

title: Saints Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael, Archangels
description: Feast of the three archangels named in Scripture.

title: Guardian Angels
description: Honors the angels assigned to protect and guide each person.

title: Saint Francis of Assisi
description: Founder of the Franciscan Order and patron of ecology.

title: Our Lady of the Rosary
description: Commemorates the victory at Lepanto and devotion to the Rosary.

title: Saint Luke, Evangelist
description: Author of the Gospel of Luke and the Acts of the Apostles.

title: Saints Simon and Jude, Apostles
description: Apostles and martyrs.

title: All Saints
description: Honors all the saints in heaven.

title: All Souls
description: Commemoration of all the faithful departed.

title: Dedication of the Lateran Basilica
description: Cathedral church of the Bishop of Rome.

title: Saint Martin of Tours, Bishop
description: Soldier, monk, and bishop known for charity.

title: Saint Cecilia, Virgin and Martyr
description: Patron saint of music and musicians.

title: Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Mary is presented in the Temple by her parents.

title: Saint Andrew, Apostle
description: Apostle, brother of Peter, and martyr.

title: Immaculate Conception of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Mary was conceived without original sin.

title: Our Lady of Guadalupe
description: Patroness of the Americas; apparition to Saint Juan Diego.

title: Saint Lucy, Virgin and Martyr
description: Virgin martyr and patron of the blind.

title: Saint John of the Cross, Priest and Doctor of the Church
description: Mystic, reformer, and Doctor of the Church.

title: Nativity of the Lord (Christmas)
description: Birth of Jesus Christ, the Son of God and Savior.

title: Saint Stephen, First Martyr
description: First Christian martyr.

title: Saint John, Apostle and Evangelist
description: Beloved disciple and author of the Fourth Gospel.

title: Holy Innocents, Martyrs
description: Children martyred by King Herod.

title: Holy Family of Jesus, Mary, and Joseph
description: Honors the family of Nazareth as a model for Christian families.

title: Saint Thomas Becket, Bishop and Martyr
description: Archbishop of Canterbury and martyr for the Church.

title: Saint Sylvester I, Pope
description: Pope during the reign of Constantine.

title: Saint Valentine, Priest and Martyr
description: Priest and martyr associated with Christian love and marriage.

title: Saint Patrick, Bishop
description: Apostle of Ireland.

title: Saint George, Martyr
description: Soldier martyr and patron of many nations.

title: Saint Benedict, Abbot
description: Father of Western monasticism and patron of Europe.

title: Saint Teresa Benedicta of the Cross (Edith Stein), Martyr
honoree_name: Saint Teresa Benedicta of the Cross
description: Philosopher, Carmelite nun, and martyr of Auschwitz.

title: Saint Rose of Lima, Virgin
description: First saint of the Americas.

title: Saint Therese of the Child Jesus, Virgin and Doctor of the Church
description: Doctor of the Church and patron of missions.

title: Saint Charles Borromeo, Bishop
description: Archbishop of Milan and reformer of the Church.

title: Saint Nicholas, Bishop
description: Bishop of Myra known for generosity.

title: Saint Lorenzo Ruiz and Companions, Martyrs
description: First Filipino saint and protomartyr of the Philippines.

title: Saint Pedro Calungsod, Martyr
description: Filipino catechist and martyr in Guam.

title: Santo Niño de Cebu
description: Popular Filipino devotion to the Holy Child Jesus of Cebu.

title: Black Nazarene (Translacion)
description: Major Filipino devotion to the suffering Christ (Quiapo, Manila).

title: Feast of the Divine Mercy
description: Devotion to Christ's mercy as revealed to Saint Faustina (also observed on Divine Mercy Sunday).

title: Saint Gabriel the Archangel
description: Messenger of the Annunciation to Mary.

title: Saint Raphael the Archangel
description: Healer and guide in the Book of Tobit.

title: Ash Wednesday
description: Beginning of Lent; day of fasting and repentance.

title: Palm Sunday of the Passion of the Lord
description: Commemorates Christ's triumphant entry into Jerusalem.

title: Holy Thursday (Evening Mass of the Lord's Supper)
description: Institution of the Eucharist and the priesthood.

title: Good Friday of the Lord's Passion
description: Commemoration of the crucifixion and death of Jesus.

title: Holy Saturday
description: Day of vigil awaiting the Resurrection.

title: Easter Sunday of the Resurrection of the Lord
description: The Lord rises from the dead.

title: Divine Mercy Sunday
description: Second Sunday of Easter; feast of Divine Mercy.

title: Ascension of the Lord
description: Christ ascends into heaven forty days after Easter.

title: Pentecost Sunday
description: Descent of the Holy Spirit upon the Apostles.

title: Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity
description: One God in three divine Persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

title: Corpus Christi (Body and Blood of Christ)
description: Solemnity of the Most Holy Body and Blood of Christ.

title: Solemnity of the Sacred Heart of Jesus
description: Devotion to the love of Jesus for humanity.

title: Our Lord Jesus Christ, King of the Universe
description: Christ the King Sunday closes the liturgical year.
